cases.title: Rank real telemetry blind spots on a service surface
cases.user_request: Audit this repo for missing observability coverage and give me the best small backlog of fixes.

notes: The repository contains several deployable surfaces with code, configs, and operational docs.
The response should stay focused on logs, metrics, traces, alerts, dashboards, and release-linked telemetry.

static_checks: Stays on observability coverage instead of drifting into general performance tuning.
review_questions: Does the response identify the specific service or workflow surfaces that matter most operationally?
Are the gaps grounded in visible repo or tooling evidence rather than generic telemetry advice?
Would the backlog be usable for a small follow-up pass?

cases.title: Stay honest when live telemetry tools are unavailable
cases.user_request: Find observability gaps in this repo, but tell me plainly if dashboards, alerts, or external metrics systems are not available.

notes: The repo has code and local configs, but external observability systems may not be connected.
The response should still rank likely gaps from repo evidence and call out what could not be verified.
available_artifacts: Partial or missing external telemetry access

static_checks: States what could not be verified because telemetry sources were unavailable.
review_questions: Does the response stay honest about missing observability sources?
Does it still extract useful evidence from the repo instead of giving up?
Are the conclusions clearly labeled as observed versus inferred?

cases.title: Model the workflow as a repeatable scheduled pass
cases.user_request: Set up a recurring observability review that finds the top telemetry gaps, uses disjoint work only when safe, and leaves a backlog for next time.

notes: The workspace is large enough that only a small batch should be handled in one pass.
Some services or packages may be separable, but shared telemetry helpers should not be split carelessly.

static_checks: Leaves a bounded backlog instead of pretending the entire workspace should be fixed at once.
review_questions: Does the response keep the pass bounded enough to repeat on a schedule?
Does it use parallel work only where the write surfaces are clearly separated?
Would the resulting backlog help improve observability over time instead of just listing problems?
